name: weights

members.name: WeightEntry
members.signature: WeightEntry(url: str, sha256: str, num_classes: int, transforms: Transform, meta: dict[str, object] = dict())

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(url: str, sha256: str, num_classes: int, transforms: Transform, meta: dict[str, object] = dict())

members.summary: Immutable manifest describing one pretrained checkpoint.
members.extended:
A `WeightEntry` is the value held by each
`WeightsEnum` member.  It is everything the runtime needs to
fetch, verify, and load a checkpoint — but nothing about the model
architecture itself (that is fixed by the factory that consumes the
entry).

parameters.name: url
parameters.description:
Direct download URL of the `model.safetensors` blob.  Lucid
hosts these on the Hugging Face Hub under the `lucid-dl` org
(`.../resolve/main/<TAG>/model.safetensors`) but no
provider-specific knowledge is encoded here — any HTTPS URL
works.

parameters.name: sha256
parameters.description:
Hex-encoded SHA-256 digest of the file at `url`.  Verified
after download and on every cache hit; a mismatch forces a
re-download (the cached copy is presumed corrupt).

parameters.name: num_classes
parameters.description:
Number of output classes the checkpoint's head produces (e.g.
`1000` for ImageNet-1k).  Lets callers sanity-check the entry
against a model's configured `num_classes` before loading.

parameters.name: transforms
parameters.description:
Preprocessing pipeline the weights were trained with — applied
to inputs at inference time so `resnet_18(pretrained=True)`
"just works".  A `lucid.utils.transforms` transform (e.g.
`lucid.utils.transforms.ImageClassification`).

parameters.name: meta
parameters.description:
Free-form provenance + metrics.  Conventional keys: `source`
(e.g. `"torchvision/ResNet18_Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1"`),
`license`, `recipe`, `metrics` (nested
`{dataset: {metric: value}}`), `num_params`, `gflops`,
`file_size_mb`.  Rendered into the Hub `config.json` +
model card by the conversion tool.

notes:
Frozen (`frozen=True`) so entries can be shared by reference and
used as `enum.Enum` values.  Two members carrying the *same*
`WeightEntry` instance collapse into an enum alias — this is how
`DEFAULT = IMAGENET1K_V1` works (see `WeightsEnum`).

members.name: WeightsEnum

methods.name: tag
methods.extended:
For the `DEFAULT` alias this resolves to the canonical
member's name (the one it aliases), not the literal string
`"DEFAULT"`.

methods.name: num_classes
methods.summary: Output-class count of this checkpoint's head.

methods.name: transforms
methods.summary: Return the preprocessing pipeline the weights expect.

returns.description:
Callable transform; apply to a `lucid.Tensor` image
before feeding the model.

examples:
>>> weights = ResNet18Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1
>>> preprocess = weights.transforms()
>>> x = preprocess(image)        # image: lucid.Tensor (C, H, W)
>>> logits = model(x[None])

members.summary: Base class for per-architecture pretrained-weight enums.
members.extended:
Each architecture subclasses `WeightsEnum` and lists its
tagged checkpoints as members whose values are `WeightEntry`
instances:

class ResNet18Weights(WeightsEnum):
        IMAGENET1K_V1 = WeightEntry(url=..., sha256=..., ...)
        DEFAULT = IMAGENET1K_V1

Members expose the underlying entry's fields directly
(`ResNet18Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1.url`) plus the tag name
(`.tag`), so call sites read naturally.  Assigning
`DEFAULT = IMAGENET1K_V1` makes `DEFAULT` an *alias* of the
canonical member (standard `enum` behaviour for duplicate
values), exactly mirroring the torchvision convention where
`DEFAULT` tracks the strongest available weights.
notes:
The `enum.Enum` value of each member is a frozen
`WeightEntry`.  Accessing `.value` returns that entry; the
convenience properties below forward to it.

members.name: download
members.signature: download(url: str, sha256: str, name: str)
members.summary: Download `url` to the local cache, verify SHA-256, return the path.
members.extended:
Idempotent — when the file already exists in cache and verifies, it
is returned without re-fetching.  A cached file that *fails*
verification is treated as corrupt and re-downloaded.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Remote location of the checkpoint blob.

parameters.name: sha256
parameters.description:
Hex-encoded expected SHA-256 digest.  Validated after download
and on every cache hit.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
Short identifier (typically `"<model>/<tag>"`) used to compose
the cache subdirectory so different checkpoints never collide.

returns.description: Local path of the verified checkpoint.

raises.description: If the freshly downloaded file fails SHA-256 verification.

From `urllib.request.urlopen` when the network is unreachable
or the URL is invalid.

notes:
Cache layout:

    {cache_root}/{name}/{sha256[:8]}/{url-basename}

Download is atomic: bytes go to a temp file in the cache directory,
the SHA is verified, then the temp file is renamed into place.  On
failure the temp file is removed; the cache directory is kept for
the next attempt.

members.name: load_weight_entry
members.signature: load_weight_entry(model: Module, weights: WeightsEnum | WeightEntry, name: str, strict: bool = True)
members.summary: Download the checkpoint described by `weights` and load it.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description:
Destination model.  Its `state_dict` keys must match the
checkpoint's (the SafeTensors file is produced by the
conversion tool against this exact key layout).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
The checkpoint to load — either an enum member
(`ResNet18Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1`) or a bare
`WeightEntry`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
Cache identifier, conventionally `"<model>/<tag>"` so each
checkpoint caches to its own directory.

parameters.name: strict
parameters.description:
Forwarded to `Module.load_state_dict`.  `False` tolerates
missing / unexpected keys (e.g. when swapping the head).

raises.description:
On SHA-256 verification failure, or on a key mismatch when
`strict=True`.

notes:
SafeTensors loading goes through
`lucid.serialization.load_safetensors` (the sanctioned
external-world bridge); this module never touches numpy directly.

members.name: resolve_weights
members.signature: resolve_weights(enum_cls: type[WeightsEnum], pretrained: bool | str, weights: WeightsEnum | None)
members.summary: Resolve a factory's `pretrained` / `weights` args to a member.
members.extended:
Implements the shared selection logic every pretrained factory uses,
so the three call styles all funnel to one enum member (or `None`
for random init).

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
`False` → no weights; `True` → the `DEFAULT` tag; a string
→ that specific tag (e.g. `"IMAGENET1K_V1"`).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description:
An explicit enum member.  Takes precedence over `pretrained`
when given.

returns.description: The selected member, or `None` for random initialisation.

raises.description:
If `weights` is from a different enum than `enum_cls`, or
`pretrained` is neither bool nor str.
If a string tag is not a member of `enum_cls`.

members.name: get_weight
members.summary: Resolve a dotted `"EnumName.TAG"` string to a weights member.

raises.description:
If the string is malformed, the enum is unknown, or the tag is
not a member of that enum.

examples:
>>> import lucid.weights as W
>>> w = W.get_weight("ResNet18Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1")
>>> w.num_classes
1000

members.name: list_pretrained
members.summary: List the available weight tags for a model.

returns.description:
Tag names (e.g. `["IMAGENET1K_V1"]`).  Empty if the model has
no registered pretrained weights.  The `DEFAULT` alias is not
listed separately — enum iteration yields only canonical
members.

members.name: register_weights
members.summary: Class decorator registering a `WeightsEnum` for discovery.

parameters.name: model_name
parameters.description: The factory name the enum belongs to (e.g. `"resnet_18"`).

returns.description:
A decorator that records the enum under both `model_name`
(for `list_pretrained`) and the enum's class name (for
`get_weight`), then returns the class unchanged.

examples:
>>> @register_weights("resnet_18")
... class ResNet18Weights(WeightsEnum):
...     IMAGENET1K_V1 = WeightEntry(...)
...     DEFAULT = IMAGENET1K_V1

parameters.name: model_name
parameters.description:
Factory name, e.g. `"resnet_18"` — the same string passed
to `register_weights` at module import time.

returns.description:
The registered enum class, or `None` when the model has
no pretrained weights declared yet.

summary: `lucid.weights` — pretrained-weight system for the model zoo.
extended:
A standalone package (sibling to `lucid.models`) that provides the
*infrastructure* for pretrained checkpoints: the tagged-variant enum
base, hub download + SHA verification, loading, and a discovery
registry.  Preprocessing lives in `lucid.utils.transforms`; a
checkpoint's `WeightEntry` carries one of those transforms.

It deliberately contains **no architecture-specific weight
declarations**.  Each model family declares its own checkpoints in a
`_weights.py` module *inside the model package* (e.g.
`lucid.models.vision.resnet._weights.ResNet18Weights`), importing the
primitives below.  This keeps `lucid.weights` a pure porting
substrate and the dependency one-directional (`models` → `weights`,
never the reverse).
notes:
Each architecture declares a `WeightsEnum` (e.g.
`ResNet18Weights`) whose members are `WeightEntry` manifests —
one per tagged checkpoint (`IMAGENET1K_V1`, …) plus a `DEFAULT`
alias.  This mirrors the torchvision `Weights` enum so call sites are
familiar:

    import lucid.models as models
    import lucid.weights as weights

    # default tag
    model = models.resnet_18(pretrained=True)

    # explicit tag — enum or string
    from lucid.models.vision.resnet import ResNet18Weights
    model = models.resnet_18_cls(weights=ResNet18Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1)
    model = models.resnet_18_cls(pretrained="IMAGENET1K_V1")

    # discover + preprocess (after the model package is imported)
    weights.list_pretrained("resnet_18")        # ['IMAGENET1K_V1']
    w = weights.get_weight("ResNet18Weights.IMAGENET1K_V1")
    x = w.transforms()(image)

The package depends only on `lucid.serialization` (for SafeTensors
loading); model factories depend on it, never the reverse, so there is
no circular import.  Crucially, `WeightEntry` carries no model
config — named factories already pin their own.

Checkpoints are hosted on the Hugging Face Hub under the `lucid-dl`
org, converted from torchvision / timm / transformers sources by the
offline `tools/convert_weights` pipeline.
